slides.title: What are RNNs?
slides.content: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) are a class of neural networks designed to recognize patterns in sequences of data such as time-series data, stock markets, and natural language.

slides.title: Architecture of RNNs
slides.content: RNNs have loops within the network, allowing information to persist over time. This structure is useful for sequential data.

slides.title: How RNNs Work
slides.content: The RNN processes data sequences step-by-step, maintaining hidden states corresponding to the sequence information.

slides.title: Applications of RNNs
slides.content: RNNs are widely used in language modeling, machine translation, speech recognition, and text generation.

slides.title: Challenges with RNNs
slides.content: RNNs face difficulties such as vanishing gradients, making it challenging to learn long-term dependencies.

slides.title: LSTM: A Solution to RNN Limitations
slides.content: Long Short-Term Memory networks (LSTMs) are designed to combat the vanishing gradient problem in RNNs.

slides.title: Summary
slides.content: RNNs are powerful for sequence prediction, but they have limitations addressed by advanced architectures like LSTMs.
